text.title: File Upload, Job Creation, and Status Polling
text.summary: OpenAI fine-tuning job lifecycle: file upload, job creation with hyperparameters, all job status states, and exponential-backoff polling pattern.

rule.statement: Validate JSONL locally before upload — the API rejects malformed files after upload, wasting time.
rule.rationale: Server-side validation error messages are non-descriptive; local validation with tiktoken and structure checks identifies issues in seconds.

rule.statement: Use the suffix parameter for model naming (e.g., "customer-support-v2") to produce reproducible, traceable model IDs across experiments.
rule.rationale: OpenAI model IDs contain a random suffix; named suffixes make the ID human-readable and traceable to the dataset version that produced it.

rule.statement: Set explicit n_epochs (2-3) for small datasets (<200 examples) — n_epochs="auto" selects too many epochs, causing overfitting.
rule.rationale: OpenAI's auto-selection calibrates for average dataset sizes; at <200 examples, "auto" often selects 5-7 epochs, well past the point of overfitting.

rule.statement: Handle all six job status values: validating_files, queued, running, succeeded, failed, cancelled — do not treat only succeeded/failed as terminal states.
rule.rationale: Missing the "cancelled" state causes a polling loop that runs indefinitely if a job is cancelled externally.

rule.statement: Use exponential backoff for polling — not a fixed 60-second sleep — to reduce API calls during short jobs and handle rate limits.
rule.rationale: Fixed 60-second sleep blocks the agent thread and hits rate limits on large polling batches; exponential backoff adapts to job duration.

rule.statement: Check job.status == "succeeded" before reading job.fine_tuned_model — the field is null until the job succeeds.
rule.rationale: Reading fine_tuned_model before job completion returns None or raises AttributeError, silently breaking the pipeline.

rule.statement: Delete training files from OpenAI Files API after training completes — uploaded files persist until explicitly deleted and count against storage limits.
rule.rationale: Undeleted files accumulate; OpenAI has per-organization storage limits, and sensitive training data should not persist on external servers beyond the training run.

rule.statement: Check file size before upload — OpenAI Files API has a 512MB limit per file; large datasets require splitting across multiple jobs.
rule.rationale: Upload fails after the entire file is transmitted if it exceeds 512MB; pre-upload size check prevents wasted upload time.

text.reference: FineTuningPipeline class with upload, train, poll, and evaluate
Token count and cost estimation before upload
